clusters.title: How hantavirus may have spread aboard a cruise ship
clusters.neutral_headline: Hantavirus may have spread between passengers on cruise ship, WHO says
bullet_summary: A suspected hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship has killed three people and sickened at least four others, officials said
Two cases of the virus, which rarely spreads between humans, have been confirmed on the ship, and three people have died
Meanwhile, the World Health Organization said it suspects some rare human to human transmission of the virus took place between close contacts on board the ship
WHO says seven confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus on MV Hondius, including three passengers who diedWhat is hantavirus, the infection thought to have killed three on cruise ship

items.title: How hantavirus may have spread aboard a cruise ship

items.content: A suspected hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship has killed three people and sickened at least four others, officials said.

items.title: Hantavirus may have spread between passengers on cruise ship, WHO says
items.standfirst: Two cases of the virus, which rarely spreads between humans, have been confirmed on the ship, and three people have died.
items.content: Two cases of the virus, which rarely spreads between humans, have been confirmed on the ship, and three people have died.

items.title: Hantavirus possibly transmitted human to human on stricken cruise ship, WHO says
items.standfirst: Passengers aboard the ship are in good spirits but may have to quarantine for "eight weeks," a World Health Organization official told CBS News.
items.content: Passengers aboard the ship are in good spirits but may have to quarantine for "eight weeks," a World Health Organization official told CBS News.

items.title: Cruise passengers tell of life on board stranded ship after hantavirus outbreak
items.standfirst: While passengers onboard the MV Hondius say the situation is calm, they face days at sea as officials warn the disease may have spread.
items.content: While passengers onboard the MV Hondius say the situation is calm, they face days at sea as officials warn the disease may have spread.

items.title: Race to find port for cruise ship as WHO traces how hantavirus spread
items.standfirst: Health authorities raced Tuesday to find a port for a cruise ship battling a hantavirus outbreak, as it remained off the West African coast with passengers isolating after three people died. Meanwhile, the World Health Organization said it suspects some rare human to human transmission of the virus took place between close contacts on board the ship.
items.content: Health authorities raced Tuesday to find a port for a cruise ship battling a hantavirus outbreak, as it remained off the West African coast with passengers isolating after three people died. Meanwhile, the World Health Organization said it suspects some rare human to human transmission of the virus took place between close contacts on board the ship.

items.title: Human-to-human hantavirus transmission suspected on board stranded cruise ship, WHO says
items.standfirst: The cruise ship carrying almost 150 people at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak was marooned in the Atlantic Ocean on Tuesday, waiting for two ill crew members to be evacuated for treatment.
items.content: The cruise ship carrying almost 150 people at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak was marooned in the Atlantic Ocean on Tuesday, waiting for two ill crew members to be evacuated for treatment.

items.title: British crew member in need of urgent medical care amid suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship
items.standfirst: WHO says seven confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus on MV Hondius, including three passengers who diedWhat is hantavirus, the infection thought to have killed three on cruise ship?A British crew member was in need of urgent medical care and a passenger from the UK remained in a critical but stable condition following a suspected outbreak of hantavirus on a luxury cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean.Three people have died and medics on Monday were scrambling to evacuate two others from the MV Hondius, which set off in March from southern Argentina carrying 149 people from 23 countries. The crisis emerged late on Sunday after the World Health Organization (WHO) said it was investigating a suspected outbreak. Continue reading...
items.content: WHO says seven confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus on MV Hondius, including three passengers who diedWhat is hantavirus, the infection thought to have killed three on cruise ship?A British crew member was in need of urgent medical care and a passenger from the UK remained in a critical but stable condition following a suspected outbreak of hantavirus on a luxury cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean.Three people have died and medics on Monday were scrambling to evacuate two others from the MV Hondius, which set off in March from southern Argentina carrying 149 people from 23 countries. The crisis emerged late on Sunday after the World Health Organization (WHO) said it was investigating a suspected outbreak. Continue reading...

items.title: Cruise ship at center of suspected deadly hantavirus outbreak refused permission to dock
items.standfirst: The cruise ship at the center of a deadly outbreak of hantavirus has been refused permission to dock, with nearly 150 people still on board — two of them seriously ill
items.content: The cruise ship at the center of a deadly outbreak of hantavirus has been refused permission to dock, with nearly 150 people still on board — two of them seriously ill

items.content: Editor’s note: This article was originally published on March 7, 2025, and has been updated to reflect the outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship

items.title: Suspected hantavirus infections leave 3 dead, several ill on a cruise ship in the Atlantic
items.standfirst: Three passengers have died and three other people are being treated amid a "public health event" involving suspected hantavirus infections on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, health officials said
items.content: Three passengers have died and three other people are being treated amid a "public health event" involving suspected hantavirus infections on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, health officials said

items.title: Three passengers dead after suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship
items.standfirst: Dutch husband and wife and third unidentified person reported to have died, with three further people taken illWhat is hantavirus, the infection thought to have killed three on cruise ship?A suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus infection on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean killed three people – including an elderly married couple – and sickened at least three others, the World Health Organisation (WHO) and South Africa’s health department said on Sunday.The WHO said an investigation was under way but that at least one case of hantavirus had been confirmed. One of the patients was in intensive care in a South African hospital, the UN’s health agency said in a statement to the Associated Press, and the WHO was working with authorities to evacuate two others with symptoms from the ship. Continue reading...
items.content: Dutch husband and wife and third unidentified person reported to have died, with three further people taken illWhat is hantavirus, the infection thought to have killed three on cruise ship?A suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus infection on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean killed three people – including an elderly married couple – and sickened at least three others, the World Health Organisation (WHO) and South Africa’s health department said on Sunday.The WHO said an investigation was under way but that at least one case of hantavirus had been confirmed. One of the patients was in intensive care in a South African hospital, the UN’s health agency said in a statement to the Associated Press, and the WHO was working with authorities to evacuate two others with symptoms from the ship. Continue reading...

Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Tuesday that the U
US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said on Tuesday that the ceasefire with Iran was not over, even as the US and Iran exchanged fire in the Gulf as they wrestled for control of the ​Strait of Hormuz

items.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire tested as U.S. guides ships through Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: Hegseth said the ceasefire with Iran "certainly holds" for now after a naval clash between Iranian and U.S. forces and renewed attacks on the UAE.
items.content: Hegseth said the ceasefire with Iran "certainly holds" for now after a naval clash between Iranian and U.S. forces and renewed attacks on the UAE.

items.title: Hegseth says Iran ceasefire is holding despite Iranian attacks on UAE, ships in Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Tuesday that the U.S.-Iran ceasefire is holding. He called Project Freedom, the effort to guide commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz that drew Iranian attacks on Monday, a separate and "temporary" operation. CBS News' Natalie Brand and Eleanor Watson have more.
items.content: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Tuesday that the U.S.-Iran ceasefire is holding. He called Project Freedom, the effort to guide commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz that drew Iranian attacks on Monday, a separate and "temporary" operation. CBS News' Natalie Brand and Eleanor Watson have more.

items.title: Is the US-Iran War Restarting?
items.standfirst: Is the US-Iran ceasefire under threat after repeat attacks in Strait of Hormuz?
items.content: Is the US-Iran ceasefire under threat after repeat attacks in Strait of Hormuz?

items.title: Trump takes questions on Iran war amid Strait of Hormuz tensions
items.standfirst: President Trump took questions on the war in Iran on Tuesday in the Oval Office after Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth gave another update on the operations in the Middle East and the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News' Aaron Navarro has the latest.
items.content: President Trump took questions on the war in Iran on Tuesday in the Oval Office after Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth gave another update on the operations in the Middle East and the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News' Aaron Navarro has the latest.

items.title: Hegseth says US ‘hasn’t capitulated on anything’ regarding Iran
items.standfirst: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told a reporter the US had not capitulated on its demands from Iran.
items.content: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told a reporter the US had not capitulated on its demands from Iran.

items.title: Hegseth 'seeking to reassure people US is still in control' in Middle East
items.standfirst: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said on Tuesday that the ceasefire with Iran was not over, even as the US and Iran exchanged fire in the Gulf as they wrestled for control of the ​Strait of Hormuz. Hegseth's comments are part of Washington's attempts to "reassure people the US is still in control" by downplaying events on the ground, says France 24's foreign affairs editor Kethevane Gorjestani.
items.content: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said on Tuesday that the ceasefire with Iran was not over, even as the US and Iran exchanged fire in the Gulf as they wrestled for control of the ​Strait of Hormuz. Hegseth's comments are part of Washington's attempts to "reassure people the US is still in control" by downplaying events on the ground, says France 24's foreign affairs editor Kethevane Gorjestani.

items.title: REPLAY: Pentagon chief Hegseth says ceasefire with Iran not over
items.standfirst: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said at a press conference on Tuesday that ​a US operation to protect commercial ships from Iran in the Strait of Hormuz is temporary, Washington is not looking ​for a ‌fight and the ceasefire with Iran ⁠is still in effect. "The ceasefire is not over," Hegseth told reporters. Watch hsi press conference in full above.
items.content: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said at a press conference on Tuesday that ​a US operation to protect commercial ships from Iran in the Strait of Hormuz is temporary, Washington is not looking ​for a ‌fight and the ceasefire with Iran ⁠is still in effect. "The ceasefire is not over," Hegseth told reporters. Watch hsi press conference in full above.

items.title: Hegseth says US blockade on Iran ‘gift to the world’
items.standfirst: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says the US has gifted the world a ‘powerful red, white, and blue dome’.
items.content: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says the US has gifted the world a ‘powerful red, white, and blue dome’.

items.title: Israeli court extends detention of two Gaza flotilla activists amid abuse claims
items.standfirst: An Israeli court has extended the detention of two foreign activists by six days after they were seized from a Gaza-bound flotilla, their lawyer said Tuesday. The pair, held in Ashkelon, deny wrongdoing as supporters allege mistreatment in custody.
items.content: An Israeli court has extended the detention of two foreign activists by six days after they were seized from a Gaza-bound flotilla, their lawyer said Tuesday. The pair, held in Ashkelon, deny wrongdoing as supporters allege mistreatment in custody.

clusters.title: Romania’s pro-EU coalition collapse 'does not alter the country's foreign policy', analyst says
clusters.neutral_headline: Romanian PM ousted in no-confidence vote
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World, Sky News World

items.title: Romania’s pro-EU coalition collapse 'does not alter the country's foreign policy', analyst says
items.standfirst: Romania's parliament on Tuesday voted to oust liberal Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan in a motion of no confidence initiated by the Social Democrats and the far right, deepening political turmoil in the EU and NATO country bordering Ukraine. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Laurențiu Pleșca, Policy Fellow at the German Marshall Fund of the United States. He says that the collapse of the government "does not alter Romania's foreign policy or security trajectory".
items.content: Romania's parliament on Tuesday voted to oust liberal Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan in a motion of no confidence initiated by the Social Democrats and the far right, deepening political turmoil in the EU and NATO country bordering Ukraine. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Laurențiu Pleșca, Policy Fellow at the German Marshall Fund of the United States. He says that the collapse of the government "does not alter Romania's foreign policy or security trajectory".

items.title: Romanian PM ousted in no-confidence vote
items.standfirst: Ilie Bolojan lost the vote after the largest party in his coalition joined the far-right opposition to depose him.
items.content: Ilie Bolojan lost the vote after the largest party in his coalition joined the far-right opposition to depose him.

items.title: Romania's pro-Europe government collapses after PM loses no-confidence vote
items.standfirst: Romania's pro-European coalition government has collapsed, after MPs voted against Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan, plunging the country into further political turmoil.
items.content: Romania's pro-European coalition government has collapsed, after MPs voted against Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan, plunging the country into further political turmoil.

items.content: The group of four activists were accused of breaking into the Elbit Systems factory near Bristol.

items.title: Four Palestine Action activists convicted of criminal damage
items.standfirst: Activists found guilty over break-in at Israeli defence firm’s UK site after jury deliberated for more than 14 hoursFour out of six Palestine Action activists who stood trial over a break-in at an Israeli defence firm’s UK site have been convicted of criminal damage.Charlotte Head, 29; Samuel Corner, 23; Leona Kamio, 30; and Fatema Rajwani, 21, were all found guilty on Tuesday of smashing up property, including drones, manufactured by Elbit Systems and computers at its factory in Filton, near Bristol, on 6 August 2024. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.standfirst: Members of the now-banned Palestine Action group raided Elbit's facility in Bristol months into Israel's war on Gaza.

clusters.title: Two dead as car rams crowd in Germany's Leipzig
clusters.neutral_headline: Two dead as car rams crowd in Germany's Leipzig
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World, NBC World

items.title: Two dead as car rams crowd in Germany's Leipzig
items.standfirst: Two ​people were killed and three others were seriously injured on Monday when a car drove into a central pedestrian zone of the eastern German city of Leipzig, Mayor Burkhard Jung ​said, the ‌latest in a spate of such incidents in recent years ⁠in Germany. Police arrested the driver, identifying him as a 33-year-old man with German citizenship.
items.content: Two ​people were killed and three others were seriously injured on Monday when a car drove into a central pedestrian zone of the eastern German city of Leipzig, Mayor Burkhard Jung ​said, the ‌latest in a spate of such incidents in recent years ⁠in Germany. Police arrested the driver, identifying him as a 33-year-old man with German citizenship.

items.title: Two killed and many injured after car driven into crowd in German city of Leipzig
items.standfirst: A 33-year-old German citizen was detained following the incident, the authorities say.
items.content: A 33-year-old German citizen was detained following the incident, the authorities say.

items.title: Two people killed as car rams into crowd in Leipzig, Germany

items.content: Two people were killed and several others were injured when a driver rammed a car into a busy pedestrian shopping street in Leipzig, Germany, on Monday, police told NBC News

clusters.title: Explosion at fireworks factory in China kills at least 26
clusters.neutral_headline: Explosion at fireworks factory in China kills at least 26
bullet_summary: The blast occurred in the city of Changsha, in Hunan province, on Monday afternoon, China’s official news agency Xinhua said
China Daily said the plant was operated by the Huasheng Fireworks Manufacturing and Display Co in the Changsha-administered, county-level city of Liuyang

items.title: Explosion at fireworks factory in China kills at least 26
items.standfirst: Other fireworks manufacturers in Liuyang, in central Hunan province, ordered to halt production after deadly blastAn explosion at a fireworks plant in a central Chinese province has killed at least 26 people and injured 61, prompting the halting of all firework manufacturing near the site.The blast occurred in the city of Changsha, in Hunan province, on Monday afternoon, China’s official news agency Xinhua said. China Daily said the plant was operated by the Huasheng Fireworks Manufacturing and Display Co in the Changsha-administered, county-level city of Liuyang. Continue reading...
items.content: Other fireworks manufacturers in Liuyang, in central Hunan province, ordered to halt production after deadly blastAn explosion at a fireworks plant in a central Chinese province has killed at least 26 people and injured 61, prompting the halting of all firework manufacturing near the site.The blast occurred in the city of Changsha, in Hunan province, on Monday afternoon, China’s official news agency Xinhua said. China Daily said the plant was operated by the Huasheng Fireworks Manufacturing and Display Co in the Changsha-administered, county-level city of Liuyang. Continue reading...

items.content: A blast at a fireworks factory in Hunan province has killed 26 people and injured 61, state media report.

clusters.title: Prosecutors to ‘fast-track’ hate crime cases in England and Wales after spate of attacks
clusters.neutral_headline: Hate crime prosecutions to be fast-tracked after antisemitic attacks
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC Top Stories

items.title: Prosecutors to ‘fast-track’ hate crime cases in England and Wales after spate of attacks
items.standfirst: Staff told to prosecute as quickly as they can, rather than waiting to gather all evidence, to tackle ‘climate of fear’ felt by Jewish communityProsecutors in England and Wales have been told to “fast-track” hate crime prosecutions after a spate of antisemitic attacks that the prime minister on Tuesday called a “crisis for all of us”.Stephen Parkinson, the director of public prosecutions, issued guidance to his staff on Tuesday telling them to bring forward prosecutions against any sort of hate crime as quickly as they could, rather than waiting until they had gathered all possible evidence. Continue reading...
items.content: Staff told to prosecute as quickly as they can, rather than waiting to gather all evidence, to tackle ‘climate of fear’ felt by Jewish communityProsecutors in England and Wales have been told to “fast-track” hate crime prosecutions after a spate of antisemitic attacks that the prime minister on Tuesday called a “crisis for all of us”.Stephen Parkinson, the director of public prosecutions, issued guidance to his staff on Tuesday telling them to bring forward prosecutions against any sort of hate crime as quickly as they could, rather than waiting until they had gathered all possible evidence. Continue reading...

items.title: Hate crime prosecutions to be fast-tracked after antisemitic attacks
items.standfirst: The director of public prosecutions has told prosecutors in England and Wales to bring charges more quickly.
items.content: The director of public prosecutions has told prosecutors in England and Wales to bring charges more quickly.

clusters.title: Zelenskyy condemns Russian ‘cynicism’ over parade truce as 23 killed in attacks
clusters.neutral_headline: Zelenskyy condemns Russian ‘cynicism’ over parade truce as 23 killed in attacks
bullet_summary: At least 12 people were killed on Tuesday in a strike on southern city of Zaporizhzhia, the regional governor said
It comes after both Moscow and Kyiv announced uniliteral ceasefires over different dates this week

items.title: Zelenskyy condemns Russian ‘cynicism’ over parade truce as 23 killed in attacks
items.standfirst: Kyiv berates Moscow’s request for ceasefire while launching ‘missile and drone strikes every single day leading up to it’Volodymyr Zelenskyy has accused the Kremlin of “utter cynicism” for seeking a truce so it can stage a military parade in Moscow as 23 people were killed in attacks on Ukraine.At least 12 people were killed on Tuesday in a strike on southern city of Zaporizhzhia, the regional governor said. “Russia ended the life of 12 people,” Ivan Fedorov posted on Telegram. Continue reading...
items.content: Kyiv berates Moscow’s request for ceasefire while launching ‘missile and drone strikes every single day leading up to it’Volodymyr Zelenskyy has accused the Kremlin of “utter cynicism” for seeking a truce so it can stage a military parade in Moscow as 23 people were killed in attacks on Ukraine.At least 12 people were killed on Tuesday in a strike on southern city of Zaporizhzhia, the regional governor said. “Russia ended the life of 12 people,” Ivan Fedorov posted on Telegram. Continue reading...

items.title: Ukraine's Zelensky slams Russian 'cynicism' ahead of separate truces
items.standfirst: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Tuesday accused Russia of "utter cynicism" for launching deadly attacks while also seeking a truce to stage its May 9 Victory Day parade in Moscow. It comes after both Moscow and Kyiv announced uniliteral ceasefires over different dates this week. France 24's Angela Diffley takes a closer look.
items.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Tuesday accused Russia of "utter cynicism" for launching deadly attacks while also seeking a truce to stage its May 9 Victory Day parade in Moscow. It comes after both Moscow and Kyiv announced uniliteral ceasefires over different dates this week. France 24's Angela Diffley takes a closer look.

clusters.title: Carney appoints former war crimes prosecutor as Canada governor general
clusters.neutral_headline: Carney appoints former war crimes prosecutor as Canada governor general
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, Financial Times

items.title: Carney appoints former war crimes prosecutor as Canada governor general
items.standfirst: Louise Arbour will serve as Canada’s representative of King Charles and carry out ceremonial and constitutional dutiesCanada’s prime minister, Mark Carney, has appointed a former supreme court justice and war crimes prosecutor as the country’s new governor general, saying her appointment would reflect the importance of global institutions.Louise Arbour, a celebrated jurist, served as United Nations commissioner and prosecuted war crimes in the former Yugoslavia and Rwanda, will serve as Canada’s representative of King Charles III. Continue reading...
items.content: Louise Arbour will serve as Canada’s representative of King Charles and carry out ceremonial and constitutional dutiesCanada’s prime minister, Mark Carney, has appointed a former supreme court justice and war crimes prosecutor as the country’s new governor general, saying her appointment would reflect the importance of global institutions.Louise Arbour, a celebrated jurist, served as United Nations commissioner and prosecuted war crimes in the former Yugoslavia and Rwanda, will serve as Canada’s representative of King Charles III. Continue reading...

items.content: In a resounding victory Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi's nationalist Hindu party has wrested control of the key state of West Bengal.

items.title: Narendra Modi’s BJP wins election in West Bengal for the first time
items.standfirst: Result in key Indian state is set to have significant implications for the country’s political landscapeNarendra Modi’s party has won a resounding election victory in West Bengal, a state which had been a rare opposition stronghold, expanding his unrivalled consolidation of power across the country.It is the first time that the Indian prime minister’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) has won assembly elections in West Bengal, a large and politically significant state in eastern India. Continue reading...

Continue reading...
